Employee.title: Employee
Employee.description: Represents an employee identified by their unique registration number in the external time clock system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Employee entity.

registrationNumber.description: The employee's unique registration number (matrícula) used to access external time clock data.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the employee record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the employee record was last updated.

MonthlyTimeRecord.title: MonthlyTimeRecord
MonthlyTimeRecord.description: Stores the scraped daily time clock data for a specific employee for a given month.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MonthlyTimeRecord entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee whose time record this is. (Relationship: Employee 1:N MonthlyTimeRecord)

year.description: The year of the monthly time record.

month.description: The month of the monthly time record (1-12).

dailyRecords.description: An array of daily time clock entries for the month. Each item details a specific day's clock events.

scrapeTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when this monthly record was last successfully scraped from the external system.

totalMonthlyHours.description: The aggregated total hours for the entire month, calculated from daily records or directly scraped.

MonthlyBalance.title: MonthlyBalance
MonthlyBalance.description: Stores an employee's hour balance for a specific month, including any manually entered previous balance and the calculated current month's cumulative balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MonthlyBalance entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee this balance belongs to. (Relationship: Employee 1:N MonthlyBalance)

year.description: The year of the balance record.

month.description: The month of the balance record (1-12).

previousMonthBalance.description: The hour balance carried over from the previous month. This can be manually entered for the first recorded month for an employee.

currentMonthCalculatedBalance.description: The calculated balance for the current month, based on the MonthlyTimeRecord data for this month.

cumulativeBalance.description: The total cumulative hour balance for the employee up to the end of this month.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when this balance record was last updated or calculated.

definition.description: Stores individual employee records. Each employee record is owned by a specific user, identified by `{userId}` in the path. The `id` field within the document should match `{employeeId}`. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `{userId}`. This structure ensures Authorization Independence for Employee data and facilitates secure QAPs.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the user who owns or manages this employee record.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific Employee record, matching the 'id' field within the document.

definition.description: Stores monthly time clock data for a specific employee. Nested under the owning user (`{userId}`) and the specific employee (`{employeeId}`). This structure ensures Authorization Independence, as the owner's UID is part of the path, and supports secure `list` queries. The `id` field within the document should match `{monthlyTimeRecordId}`. Includes denormalized 'employeeId' for easy reference within the document, although it's also present in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the user who owns the parent employee record.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Employee record.

params.name: monthlyTimeRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the monthly time record, matching the 'id' field within the document.

definition.description: Stores monthly hour balance data for a specific employee. Nested under the owning user (`{userId}`) and the specific employee (`{employeeId}`). This structure ensures Authorization Independence, as the owner's UID is part of the path, and supports secure `list` queries. The `id` field within the document should match `{monthlyBalanceId}`. Includes denormalized 'employeeId' for easy reference within the document, although it's also present in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID of the user who owns the parent employee record.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Employee record.

params.name: monthlyBalanceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the monthly balance record, matching the 'id' field within the document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and secure `list` operations (QAPs) by leveraging path-based ownership and structural segregation. All user-specific data is nested under `/users/{userId}/`. This approach ensures that the Firebase `request.auth.uid` can be directly compared against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path for all authorization checks, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For example, to access any `Employee` record, `MonthlyTimeRecord`, or `MonthlyBalance`, the authenticated user's ID must match the `{userId}` in the path. This provides atomic access control and simplifies rule logic significantly.

Specifically, `Employee` documents are stored under `/users/{userId}/employees/{employeeId}`, where `{userId}` denotes the user who owns or manages this employee record. `MonthlyTimeRecord` and `MonthlyBalance` entities, which are conceptually children of an `Employee`, are further nested under their respective `Employee` document: `/users/{userId}/employees/{employeeId}/monthlyTimeRecords/{monthlyTimeRecordId}` and `/users/{userId}/employees/{employeeId}/monthlyBalances/{monthlyBalanceId}`. This hierarchical structure ensures that the `ownerId` (represented by `{userId}`) is implicitly present in the path of all related subcollection documents, thus achieving authorization independence for these nested entities without needing to denormalize the `ownerId` field directly into every document. This also ensures that `list` queries (QAPs) are inherently secure, as a user can only query within their own `/users/{userId}/` subtree, preventing unauthorized data enumeration.
